title: Duplicate Items without automatically Inserting "(copy)" in the Project Field
excerpt: I use duplicates everyday and quite often because we repeat several projects on various dates for every client. Unfortunately, when I dupe an item it adds the word “copy” and adds additional "copy"s to the same Project f…

posts.author: Hollywood_Jeff (Jeff Peri)
posts.cookedHtml: I use duplicates everyday and quite often because we repeat several projects on various dates for every client. Unfortunately, when I dupe an item it adds the word “copy” and adds additional "copy"s to the same Project field each time I duplicate. So as an example, an Item named “Project A” will add the text “copy” at the end like so, Project A (copy) and every subsequent dupe will add an additional “(copy)” like so, “Project A (copy) (copy)” and so on. There should be a setting to option out of adding the word “copy” in the field. I was recommend to use another app but, frankly I don’t want to pay extra for an app for something that should be an option. Here is the original discussion: How do I stop "(copy)" from being included in my Item duplicates?

posts.author: AnishJ (Anish)
posts.cookedHtml: you can try Remove Copy

posts.author: Zaur158 (Zaur)
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah, that would make a lot of sense, Jeff. Having “(copy)” stack up automatically every time you copy something can really become a mess, particularly if you’re dealing with duplicate projects. Having a toggle or checkbox for turning that on and off in the settings would surely be a benefit. Currently, the only current workaround is to manually rename the item every time or script/automation-strip out “(copy)” upon duplication, which isn’t perfect. Hopefully, they include that as a native feature in the near future — it’s one of those minor quality-of-life adjustments that would take so much time off for individuals duplicating projects constantly.

posts.author: Hollywood_Jeff (Jeff Peri)
posts.cookedHtml: That is certainly an option. We have several standardized items for projects (Groups) so ironically I used Excel creating lists of Items and simply paste them into the Group. It took a while to set up but it is a work around to copying Items repeatedly. And, I do not have to delete the dreaded “(copy)”. I’m surprised they haven’t solved this issue yet.

posts.author: AnishJ (Anish)
posts.cookedHtml: Interesting nice idea, because they know people always found clever ways to fix things up haha
